input.prompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

data.content:
## Migrating MSTest 3.8.0 to Microsoft.Testing.Platform

### 1. Add a `Directory.Build.props` (shared across all projects)

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <UseTestingPlatform>true</UseTestingPlatform>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

- **`UseTestingPlatform`** – switches MSTest to run on MTP instead of VSTest.
- **`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`** – keeps `dotnet test` working (it bridges to MTP under the hood). Required on .NET 8; default-on starting with .NET 9+.

### 2. Per-project changes

No `.csproj` changes needed if you use `Directory.Build.props`. Just ensure each project already references:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
```

(The `MSTest` meta-package at 3.8.0 already bundles the MTP runner — no extra package needed.)

### 3. CI command

```bash
# Before (VSTest)
dotnet test --logger trx

# After (MTP)
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

The command stays the same — `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` ensures `dotnet test` still works. The `--logger trx` flag is forwarded to MTP's TRX report generator. Adding `--results-directory` is optional but recommended for predictable output paths.

### Summary of what each property does

| Property | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `UseTestingPlatform` | Opts MSTest into MTP runner |
| `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` | Bridges `dotnet test` → MTP (needed on .NET 8) |

That's it — no test code changes, no extra NuGet packages, and your CI pipeline command remains `dotnet test --logger trx`.
